capabilities: Ensure ETL processes are idempotent to allow safe retries without data duplication or corruption.

procedure.action: Extract source data using a SELECT query with a consistent snapshot isolation level to ensure data consistency across retries.

Rewrite data by applying deterministic functions that produce the same output for the same input, ensuring idempotency by applying mapping rules from source schema to target schema; assert output_record_count = input_record_count; assert dropped_record_count < 1.
Load transformed data into the target system using UPSERT operations to avoid duplicate entries and ensure idempotency.

Log each ETL operation with a unique operation ID and status to facilitate tracking and retries in case of failures using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Invoke a retry mechanism with exponential backoff using a retry library to handle transient errors during ETL operations following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.

summary: Implementing idempotent ETL pipelines ensures that data extraction, transformation, and loading processes can be safely retried without causing duplication or data corruption. This pattern is crucial for maintaining data integrity during migrations or regular synchronizations.
title: Idempotent ETL Pipeline Pattern for Reliable Data Migration

failureConditions: Data duplication occurs when ETL operations are not idempotent, causing multiple entries for the same data.
Data corruption happens when transformations are non-deterministic, leading to inconsistent results across retries.
System overload occurs when retries are not properly managed, causing excessive load on the source or target systems.
metrics: test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
no duplicate primary keys in target after ETL completion
retry success rate > 95% over 1000 ETL operations
successConditions: data_integrity_check: assert no duplicate primary keys in target after ETL completion.
transformation_determinism: run transformation function twice on sample data; assert hash(output1) == hash(output2).
retry_success_rate: assert retry success rate > 95% over 1000 ETL operations.
